metadata.title: The Infinite Square Well
metadata.notes: Film six — 'the notes it's allowed to play.' ARCHIVE STAR: the 1993 quantum corral, cold open AND full-circle closer (B01/B09, same artifact). No bio kicker (relevance-gated — the corral return is the kicker). Tangent mid-film on E_n = n²π²ℏ²/2mL². CHAPTER ERRATUM caught at Gate F: marble energy is ~3×10⁻⁴² eV, not 10⁻⁴³ (10× slip in the chapter's arithmetic); narration corrected. Scope: quantization from confinement; finite wells/tunneling → ch 6.
chapters: The guitar string, and where it breaks
The bottom rung is never zero
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: In 1993, IBM scientists arranged forty-eight iron atoms into a ring, then photographed the space inside. Concentric ripples. Nobody drew them — confinement did.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In nineteen ninety-three, IBM scientists arranged forty-eight iron atoms into a ring, then photographed the space inside. Concentric ripples. Nobody drew them — confinement did.

shot.note: AI recreation of the corral STM data (i2v from the authentic image) — disclosed; the real photograph is B09

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO·archive] Crommie–Lutz–Eigler quantum corral STM image (IBM, 1993) — set shot.focus on the interior rings; sidecar with Science 262, 218 citation + IBM credit (NOT PD — commentary use, see SHOTLIST)

beats.narration_text: Last film: only waves that fit survive. This film: trap a particle in a box, and count the notes it's allowed to play.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Last film: only waves that fit survive. This film: trap a particle in a box, and count the notes it's allowed to play.

beats.narration_text: A guitar string pinned at both ends plays only whole humps — one, two, three. Quantum swaps the string for probability itself. Same walls. Different thing waving.
beats.tts_normalized_text: A guitar string pinned at both ends plays only whole humps — one, two, three. Quantum swaps the string for probability itself. Same walls. Different thing waving.

viz.data: String between two pins: modes 1, 2, 3 draw on in turn (ink); a fractional mode (crimson, 1⅓ humps) enters, misses the right pin, fades. Then the swap: string relabels to |ψ|² chip (navy) — 'same walls, different thing waving'
viz.note: The analogy AND its break in one beat — the chapter's warning is the point, not a footnote

beats.narration_text: Schrödinger's equation in the box allows any energy, until the walls speak. The wave must die at both ends: sine of k L equals zero. Whole numbers only.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Schrödinger's equation in the box allows any energy, until the walls speak. The wave must die at both ends: sine of k L equals zero. Whole numbers only.

viz.data: Continuous k-dial (any energy) sweeping; boundary chips ψ(0)=0, ψ(L)=0 land; the dial COLLAPSES to discrete ticks as 'sin(kL) = 0 → kL = nπ' card stamps in (slate card, crimson n)
viz.note: Quantization enters with this single equation — the collapse from continuum to ticks is the drama

beats.narration_text: The energy menu in one line: n squared times a floor price, set by the particle's mass and the width of its box. Nothing in between.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The energy menu in one line: n squared times a floor price, set by the particle's mass and the width of its box. Nothing in between.

tangent.lhs: The energy of note number n.
tangent.rhs: n squared, times the confinement floor price.
tangent.claim: Confinement writes the menu — whole numbers only.

glossary.mean: why there's a floor at all
heavier particle or roomier box — lower notes

example.scenario: Electron in L = 1 nm, versus a 1 g marble in L = 1 cm.
example.verdict: same law — loud for electrons, silent for marbles
example.cost: The classical world is arithmetic, not a different universe.

beats.narration_text: n counts the humps: one, two, three. h-bar makes it quantum. Heavier particle or wider box — every note in the menu drops.
beats.tts_normalized_text: n counts the humps: one, two, three. h-bar makes it quantum. Heavier particle or wider box — every note in the menu drops.

beats.narration_text: An electron in a one-nanometer box: level one costs point three-eight electron volts — chemistry's scale. A marble in a centimeter box: ten to the minus forty-two. Invisible.
beats.tts_normalized_text: An electron in a one-nano-meter box: level one costs point three-eight electron volts — chemistry's scale. A marble in a centimeter box: ten to the minus forty-two. Invisible.

viz.note: Marble figure is the GATE-F CORRECTED value (3×10⁻⁴² eV) — chapter's 10⁻⁴³ is a 10× arithmetic slip, erratum filed

beats.narration_text: In 1913, Bohr decreed quantization — a postulate. In 1926, Schrödinger derived it from a differential equation and two boundary conditions. Nature's whole numbers, explained.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In nineteen thirteen, Bohr decreed quantization — a postulate. In nineteen twenty-six, Schrödinger derived it from a differential equation and two boundary conditions. Nature's whole numbers, explained.
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION·archive] side-by-side PD portraits: Bohr (~1913) and Schrödinger (~1926), Wikimedia Commons; chips 'DECREED · 1913' / 'DERIVED · 1926' land at assembly (Remotion plane; degrades to clean plate). Sidecars with Commons URLs — real archives, real people, no AI

beats.narration_text: The levels climb as n squared — one, four, nine, sixteen. Each higher note adds one more zero-crossing: shorter wave, faster particle, higher rung.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The levels climb as n squared — one, four, nine, sixteen. Each higher note adds one more zero-crossing: shorter wave, faster particle, higher rung.

viz.data: Left: well with modes n=1..4 stacked, node dots (crimson) marked on each — 0, 1, 2, 3 nodes; right: energy ladder with rungs at 1, 4, 9, 16 (mono chips), spacing visibly widening
viz.note: Node count n−1 = Fourier logic you can see; the widening ladder IS the n² claim

beats.narration_text: And the bottom rung is never zero. A trapped particle can't sit still — stillness plus confinement violates the uncertainty principle. Being boxed in costs energy.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And the bottom rung is never zero. A trapped particle can't sit still — stillness plus confinement violates the uncertainty principle. Being boxed in costs energy.

viz.data: Energy axis with E=0 line (dashed); a crimson X stamps on E=0; E₁ rung lands above it with gap highlighted; side card: 'still + confined → σₓσₚ = 0 < ℏ/2' struck through (hand strike, one pen voice)
viz.note: Kennard argument compressed: E=0 ⇒ σ_p=0, but σ_x ≤ L — contradiction; ch 9's film owns the full inequality

beats.narration_text: Mix the two lowest notes and the probability sloshes across the box every three point seven femtoseconds. Motion is the beat between quantum notes.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Mix the two lowest notes and the probability sloshes across the box every three point seven femtoseconds. Motion is the beat between quantum notes.

viz.data: Well with 1+2 superposition density sloshing (cos cross term, ≥2 full periods); mono chip 'T ≈ 3.7 fs' lands on the first return; echo of film five's B08 — deliberate series rhyme
viz.note: T = h/3E₁ = 3.67 fs for the 1 nm electron — COMPUTED, matches chapter's 3.66

beats.narration_text: Those rings at IBM are this exact mathematics, photographed — standing waves of probability, penned by forty-eight atoms. The walls impose the notes. Nature obeys.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Those rings at IBM are this exact mathematics, photographed — standing waves of probability, penned by forty-eight atoms. The walls impose the notes. Nature obeys.
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO·archive] the corral again — closer crop on the interior rings than B01 (full circle, not a repeat); same sidecar; annotation ring on the standing-wave rings lands at assembly (Remotion plane)

beats.narration_text: Next: real walls are never infinitely tall. Soften them, and particles begin to leak straight through. Tunneling.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: real walls are never infinitely tall. Soften them, and particles begin to leak straight through. Tunneling.
